questions.question: A chord $AB$ of a circle of radius $15 \text{ cm}$ subtends an angle of $60^\circ$ at the centre. Find the areas of the corresponding minor and major segments of the circle. (Use $\pi = 3.14$ and $\sqrt{3} = 1.73$)
questions.answer: Area of minor segment = $20.4375 \text{ cm}^2$; Area of major segment = $686.0625 \text{ cm}^2$

steps: Identify the radius $r = 15$ and central angle $\theta = 60^\circ$.
Calculate the area of sector $OAB = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2$.
Calculate the area of $\triangle OAB$. Since $\theta = 60^\circ$ and $OA = OB$, it is an equilateral triangle, so Area $= \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} r^2$.
Subtract the triangle area from the sector area to find the minor segment.
Subtract the minor segment area from the total circle area to find the major segment.
solution.derivation: Area of sector $= \frac{60}{360} \times 3.14 \times 15^2 = \frac{1}{6} \times 3.14 \times 225 = 117.75 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Area of } \triangle OAB = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 15^2 = 0.4325 \times 225 = 97.3125 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Minor segment} = 117.75 - 97.3125 = 20.4375 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Area of circle} = 3.14 \times 15^2 = 706.5 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Major segment} = 706.5 - 20.4375 = 686.0625 \text{ cm}^2.

questions.question: An umbrella has 8 ribs which are equally spaced. Assuming the umbrella to be a flat circle of radius $45 \text{ cm}$, find the area between the two consecutive ribs of the umbrella.

steps: Determine the total number of sectors formed by 8 ribs, which is 8.
Calculate the central angle of each sector: $\theta = 360^\circ / 8 = 45^\circ$.
Use the formula for the area of a sector: $A = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2$.
Substitute $r = 45$ and $\theta = 45^\circ$ into the formula.
Simplify the expression to get the final area.

questions.question: Find the area of the shaded region in a square $ABCD$ of side $14 \text{ cm}$, where four circles are drawn with centers at the four vertices of the square, each having a radius of $7 \text{ cm}$.

steps: Calculate the area of the square $ABCD = \text{side}^2$.
Identify that each corner forms a quadrant of a circle of radius $7 \text{ cm}$.
Calculate the sum of the areas of the four quadrants, which is equivalent to the area of one full circle of radius $7 \text{ cm}$.
Subtract the area of the circle from the area of the square.
The remaining area is the shaded region.
solution.derivation: Area of square $= 14 \times 14 = 196 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Area of 4 quadrants} = 4 \times (\frac{1}{4} \pi r^2) = \pi r^2 = \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7 = 154 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Shaded area} = 196 - 154 = 42 \text{ cm}^2.

questions.question: A round table cover has six equal designs as shown in the figure. If the radius of the cover is $28 \text{ cm}$, find the cost of making the designs at the rate of $\text{ ₹ } 0.35 \text{ per cm}^2$. (Use $\sqrt{3} = 1.7$)

steps: Recognize that each of the 6 designs forms a segment of a circle.
Central angle for each segment is $360^\circ / 6 = 60^\circ$.
Calculate the area of one segment: Area of sector - Area of equilateral triangle.
Multiply the area of one segment by 6 to get the total area of the designs.
Multiply the total area by the rate to find the cost.
solution.derivation: Area of one segment $= \frac{60}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 28^2 - \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 28^2 = \frac{1}{6} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 784 - 1.7 \times 196 = 410.66 - 333.2 = 77.46 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Total Area} = 6 \times 77.46 = 464.76 \text{ cm}^2. \text{ Cost} = 464.76 \times 0.35 = 162.666 \approx 162.68 \text{ ₹}.
